perturbation.original_text: that the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members of the Association, unless he/she has clear instruction from the Executive Director or Board Chairperson to do so, and then shall disclose such information only for the limited purpose for which such instruction is given and no other purpose
perturbation.changed_text: that the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members of the Association, unless he/she has instruction to do so, and then shall disclose such information only for the limited purpose for which such instruction is given and no other purpose
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'clear instruction from the Executive Director or Board Chairperson', this change introduces ambiguity as to who can provide the instruction to disclose confidential information. Originally the text specifies that instruction must come from a very limited set of people (Executive Director or Board Chairperson), this change means the instruction can come from anyone, potentially contradicting the overall intent of the agreement to protect confidential information, which is referenced in the following sections
perturbation.contradicted_text: (5) that the Recipient is aware of and has reviewed the ICoCA’s Conflict of Interest and Information Security policies and that Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person within the Association (i.e., the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members) if the disclosure of such information would violate the Association’s Conflict of Interest or Information Security policies
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted disclosure instructions to the Executive Director or Board Chairperson, reinforcing information security. The changed text broadens this, allowing instruction from anyone. The contradicted text states that recipients shouldn't disclose confidential information even to members of the Association if it violates the Conflict of Interest or Information Security policies. By broadening who can provide instruction, the changed text creates a direct contradiction, as someone outside of leadership (who might not be as aware of conflict of interest policies) could instruct the recipient to disclose information in a way that does violate those policies, thus causing a strong in-text contradiction according to the Omissions definition, as essential language has been removed creating a contradiction with constraints in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Definition of Confidential Information For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information includes, but is not limited to, financial or other competitive business information that may be collected by the Association regarding industry members (and non-members) of the Association, information of a personal nature regarding any of th e officers, staff, or employees of the Association or its members, information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions (unless such information has been specifically cleared for public release), and any other information which might be reasonably foreseen to be harmful if disclosed outside of the Association and/or for reasons other than in accordance with a D irector’s, Officer’s, or Staff Member ’s duties to the Association. In the case that the Recipient is unclear or has any question regarding whether or not a particular piece of information falls within Confidential Information, the Recipient will consult with the Executive Director or Office Manager prior to disclosing such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Definition of Confidential Information For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information includes information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions. In the case that the Recipient is unclear or has any question regarding whether or not a particular piece of information falls within Confidential Information, the Recipient will consult with the Executive Director or Office Manager prior to disclosing such information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the broad definition of Confidential Information ('but is not limited to, financial or other competitive business information... any other information which might be reasonably foreseen to be harmful if disclosed outside of the Association'), the text now narrowly defines it. This creates an in-text contradiction because the primary purpose refers to sharing information and protecting Confidential Information. Now, the scope of protected information is significantly reduced, potentially making the 'Primary Purpose' section contradictory or nonsensical in some cases.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Primary Purpose The Primary Purpose of the Agreement is to enable the sharing of information between the Association and the Recipient in a manner that enables the proper functioning of the Association and the full participation of members while also providing access to Confidential Information (as defined in this Agreement) and the responsibilities to ensure that such information is not disseminated or used inappropriately.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' broadly, including but not limited to specific examples. The changed text significantly narrows this definition, focusing solely on information shared in meetings. This creates a direct contradiction with the 'Primary Purpose' section, which states the agreement's purpose is to enable the sharing of information while protecting Confidential Information (as defined in this Agreement). Because the change narrows the definition and the primary purpose refers to the sharing and protecting of confidential information it leads to uncertainty in which information is considered confidential. The broad nature is removed and now the contract only refers to information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is entered into as of _______________, ____ between __________________________ (“Recipient”) and the International Code of Conduct for Private Security Service Providers’ Association (the “ICoCA” or the “Association ”)(together the Parties)
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is entered into as of _______________, ____ between __________________________ (“Recipient”) and the International Code of Conduct for Private Security Service Providers’ Association (the “ICoCA” or the “Association ”)
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(together the Parties)', the text now introduces an in-text contradiction when reffering to 'the Parties' throughout the agreement, like '(b) Where the Parties have a mandatory, legally binding obligation which requires disclosure of Confidential Information'. Now it's unclear who 'the Parties' are because this term wasn't defined, so its contradicting itself in multiple sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Where the Parties have a mandatory, legally binding obligation which requires disclosure of Confidential Information
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'the Parties' as 'Recipient' and 'ICoCA'. The changed text removes this definition. The contradicted text refers to 'the Parties'. Without a clear definition of 'the Parties', the contradicted text becomes ambiguous and creates an in-text contradiction, as it is no longer clear to whom the obligations apply.

perturbation.original_text: Primary Purpose The Primary Purpose of the Agreement is to enable the sharing of information between the Association and the Recipient in a manner that enables the proper functioning of the Association and the full participation of members while also providing access to Confidential Information (as defined in this Agreement) and the responsibilities to ensure that such information is not disseminated or used inappropriately.
perturbation.changed_text: Primary Purpose The Primary Purpose of the Agreement is to enable the sharing of information between the Association and the Recipient in a manner that enables the proper functioning of the Association and the full participation of members.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'while also providing access to Confidential Information (as defined in this Agreement) and the responsibilities to ensure that such information is not disseminated or used inappropriately.', the section now has a new intext contradiction because other sections references and mentions the importance of Confidential Information in the context of the Agreement, but this section no longer even acknowledges it. This introduces a contradiction in intent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (1) that in the normal course of business of the Association, there may be times when Recipient, as a Director, Officer, or Staff Member of the Association, receives information that is confidential, sensitive, and/or not in the public domain and that, if disclosed to persons other than those for whom the information as intended, has the potential to harm the Association, its individual members, other participants, or any of their business partners or related entities or persons (such information her eafter referred to as “Confidential Information”)
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement's primary purpose is to enable information sharing while also addressing confidential information and related responsibilities. The changed text removes the section about confidential information. This creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' explicitly defines and emphasizes the importance of 'Confidential Information' within the context of the Association's business. Removing the initial mention of confidential information in the 'Primary Purpose' clause creates a strong contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text', which relies on the assumption that the agreement covers and acknowledges confidential information. This aligns with the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer, the violation of which will permit the Association to terminate such employment or terminate such person from his7her position in addition to seeking any other legal or equitable remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer, the violation of which will permit the Association to terminate such employment or terminate such person from his7her position.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'in addition to seeking any other legal or equitable remedies.', the section creates a contradiction, since in (6) section, also the 'legal and/or equitable' was removed, now those sections are contradicting each other since those legal and/or equitable remedies are the bases for enforcing this agreement. If no legal and/or equitable remedies are specified elsewhere, this renders the termination clause potentially unenforceable, thereby contradicting the agreement's overall aim of enforcing confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) that in the event Recipient violates any part of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement, the Association may pursue remedies from Recipient
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that violating the Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement permits the Association to terminate employment and seek other legal or equitable remedies. The changed text removes the phrase 'in addition to seeking any other legal or equitable remedies.' The contradicted text states that the Association may pursue remedies from Recipient if they violate the agreement. The removal creates a contradiction because the 'remedies' are essential for enforcing the agreement, and removing the specification of 'legal or equitable remedies' weakens the Association's ability to enforce the agreement, thereby conflicting with the contradicted text which assumes remedies are available and actionable. The explanation confirms that this is an Omissions-In Text Contradiction.
